Defendants Janet Krahling and Pacific Union International, Inc. contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JANET KRAHLING AND PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden is show that the release in the consolidated class action is applicable to the case at bar, including evidence and argument that the claims in this action (failure to disclose sinking/tilting in March 2016 in a sale of a single unit) are factually related to the claims asserted in any consolidate action, e.g. no reference to specific causes of action or claims for comparison. Reyan's Pasta Bella v. Visa USA (2006) 442 F. 3d 741. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. Triable issues of fact as to the knowledge and failure to disclose sinking/tilting. Objection to Sean Jeffries deposition transcript is sustained.

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. SAIED KASHANI, Esq. (Tel: 213-625-4320) for plaintiff;
Atty. WILLIAM D. WHITEMAN, Esq. of Law Offices of William D. Whiteman (Tel: 415-989-3300) for defendant Robert A. Pike.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ROBERT PIKE's Motion To Compel Responses.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

On January 19, 2021 the parties agreed to a hearing date on a motion to compel discovery responses of February 18, 2021. During the same conversation, moving party admits that opposing counsel said he would provide responses "tomorrow" which was January 20, 2021. Pursuant to CCP section 1005(b), the motion to compel must be served and filed 16 court days before the hearing date. Here that date would be January 25, 2021. Counsel had adequate time to wait and see if the responses were forthcoming on January 20, 2021 as promised before preparing the motion. Indeed, responses were provided on January 20, 2021 as promised. Motion for sanctions DENIED.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at Peter.VanZandt@lewisbrisbois.com.
